activities_projects.title: Critical Analysis of Wit in Leadership
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the role of wit and intelligence in leadership and governance
steps: Read the story carefully and identify how Tenali Rama uses wit to influence the king
Discuss in groups how humour and intelligence can help leaders make better decisions
Compare Tenali Rama with another historical or fictional advisor known for wisdom
Prepare a short presentation explaining your findings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop critical thinking about leadership qualities and the role of intelligence in governance
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of analysis and quality of presentation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Interpretation of Riddles and Symbolism
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the deeper meaning and symbolism in riddles
steps: Read the riddles given in the chapter
Identify the literal and symbolic meanings of each riddle
Explain why riddles require wit and creativity to solve
Create one original riddle and share it with the class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop interpretation skills and creative thinking
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of explanation and originality of riddle (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Debate: Humour vs Authority
activities_projects.objective: Examine the balance between humour and respect in authority relationships
steps: Divide the class into two groups
One group argues that humour should always be allowed in royal courts or leadership settings
The other group argues that humour can sometimes undermine authority
Conduct a structured debate and conclude with a class discussion
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop debating skills and understand different perspectives
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of arguments and use of examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Strategic Thinking Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyse Tenali Rama’s strategy and apply it to modern problem-solving
steps: Study the court scene where Tenali Rama presents the paddy seeds
Identify the logical steps in his strategy
Explain how indirect communication helped the king realise his mistake
Apply the same strategy to solve a modern hypothetical problem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students learn strategic thinking and indirect persuasion techniques
activities_projects.evaluation: Logical explanation and application of strategy (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Creative Courtroom Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Encourage creative interpretation of the story through performance
steps: Recreate the royal court setting in the classroom
Students play roles such as king, queen, Tenali Rama, ministers, and courtiers
Extend the story by imagining another conflict in the court
Use wit and humour to resolve the imagined conflict
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students improve creativity, improvisation, and collaborative skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity, teamwork, and clarity of performance (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Language and Expression Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Examine how descriptive expressions shape the tone of the story
steps: Identify expressive phrases such as 'stormed off' and 'hush fell over'
Analyse how these expressions influence the emotional tone of the narrative
Rewrite a selected paragraph using alternative expressions
Compare the tone before and after rewriting
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the importance of word choice in storytelling
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of analysis and rewriting (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Narrative Structure Examination
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the structure of the story including conflict, climax, and resolution
steps: Identify the introduction, rising conflict, climax, and resolution in the story
Explain how the misunderstanding creates tension
Describe how Tenali Rama resolves the conflict
Create a diagram showing the story’s narrative structure
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand narrative techniques used in storytelling

activities_projects.title: Advanced Grammar Application
activities_projects.objective: Apply conditional sentence structures in complex situations
steps: Study the conditional sentence examples in the chapter
Write five complex conditional sentences related to the story
Rewrite them by changing the tense or condition
Explain how the meaning changes
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct grammar usage and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparative Literature Activity
activities_projects.objective: Compare Tenali Rama with other witty characters in literature
steps: Research another witty character such as Birbal or Nasreddin Hodja
Compare their problem-solving methods with Tenali Rama
Identify similarities and differences
Write a short comparative report
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of comparison and supporting examples (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Reflective Essay on Wisdom and Humour
activities_projects.objective: Encourage reflective thinking about the role of wit in society
steps: Reflect on situations where humour helped resolve conflicts
Relate those experiences to the story of Tenali Rama
Write an essay discussing how wit can promote understanding and harmony
Conclude with lessons learned from the story
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students develop reflective writing and critical thinking skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of ideas, structure, and originality (10 marks)
